annual_deductible.notes: Per-claim deductible of ₹25,000 applies to each hospitalisation claim separately (not aggregate). Policyholder pays the first ₹25,000 of each distinct hospitalisation event. This differs from Plan A which uses an aggregate annual deductible. Per-claim deductible is common in mid-range plans from Niva Bupa and Star Health.
annual_deductible.source: Niva Bupa ReAssure 2.0 and Star Health Comprehensive plan structures. Beshak.org plan data (April 2026). HDFC ERGO Optima Secure policy wording defines both aggregate and per-claim deductible types. https://www.beshak.org/insurance/health-insurance/best-health-insurance-plans/niva-bupa-re-assure-2-0-platinum/

coinsurance.notes: Insurer pays 75% of admissible claim amount after deductible is applied. Policyholder bears 25%. Slightly lower than Plan A's 80% to reflect lower sum insured and per-claim deductible structure. 75/25 coinsurance is representative of mid-tier plans in India.
coinsurance.source: Star Health Comprehensive, Care Advantage plan coinsurance structures. Representative value based on Policybazaar plan comparison data and Beshak.org ratings (April 2026).

copay.notes: 10% co-payment applies when treatment is taken at a non-network hospital. No co-payment at network hospitals for members under 60. Senior citizens (60+) face 20% co-payment regardless of network status. Co-payment is calculated on admissible claim amount after deductible.
copay.source: Niva Bupa copay policy page (https://www.nivabupa.com/health-insurance/copay-health-insurance.html). Star Health non-network co-payment structure. Common in mid-range plans to discourage non-network hospital usage.

out_of_pocket_max.notes: OOP max of ₹1,50,000 per policy year (lower than Plan A due to lower sum insured). Once cumulative patient out-of-pocket (deductibles + coinsurance shares) reaches ₹1,50,000, insurer covers 100% of further admissible claims. OOP max is tracked annually and resets at renewal.
out_of_pocket_max.source: Modelled on Care Supreme and ICICI Lombard Complete Health plan OOP cap structures. IRDAI does not mandate OOP caps but allows them under Insurance Products Regulations 2024.

network_rules.non_network_reimbursement_notes: For non-network hospitalisation, policyholder pays upfront and files reimbursement within 15 days of discharge. Reimbursement is 75% of admissible expenses minus 10% co-payment on non-network usage. Room rent at non-network hospitals capped at ₹3,000/day for standard room.
network_rules.room_rent_network_notes: No sub-limit on room rent at network hospitals under this plan.
network_rules.source: Niva Bupa 30-minute pre-auth commitment (https://www.nivabupa.com/). IRDAI Master Circular 2024: cashless discharge within 3 hours. Network hospital count based on Niva Bupa's 10,000+ network and Star Health's 14,000+ network; 8,000 is a conservative mid-range figure.

covered_services.notes: Covered up to sum insured for minimum 24-hour admission. All standard hospitalisation components included.
Covered after 24-month waiting period for pre-existing conditions. Acute injury not classified as pre-existing: covered from day 1 of policy. Pre-authorisation mandatory for all planned surgeries.
Same waiting period and preauth rules as ACL. Covered in combined procedures (arthroscopy with ACL + meniscus).

Post-hospitalisation physiotherapy covered for up to 60 days after discharge, subject to ₹20,000 sub-limit per claim episode. Lower sub-limit than Plan A reflects lower sum insured base. Standalone OPD physiotherapy not covered without linked admission.
covered_services.source: Star Health Comprehensive post-hospitalisation benefit covers 60 days. Sub-limit of ₹20,000 modelled on Star Health and Care Health PT reimbursement caps.

covered_services.notes: All IRDAI-listed day care procedures covered. Arthroscopic ACL repair commonly performed as day care.
30-day pre-hospitalisation window (shorter than Plan A's 60-day window), reflecting standard mid-tier plan terms.

exclusions.notes: OPD-only physiotherapy not covered. Must be linked to an inpatient or day care admission.

exclusions.exclusion: Pre-existing orthopaedic conditions within 24-month waiting period
exclusions.notes: Any ACL, meniscus or knee condition documented before policy start is subject to 24-month waiting period.
Non-medical consumables, registration charges, attendant charges not reimbursed at non-network hospitals.
Treatments not approved by relevant medical regulatory bodies.
Bariatric surgery excluded unless clinically necessary and specifically covered by add-on.

documents_required: Referral letter from general physician or orthopaedic specialist
preauthorization.emergency_process: Hospital calls insurer helpline within 24 hours of emergency admission. Cashless authorisation is issued within 1 hour of document receipt.
preauthorization.source: Niva Bupa: 30-minute pre-auth turnaround commitment for all active policies (https://www.nivabupa.com/). IRDAI Master Circular 2024: 1 hour for cashless authorisation; 3 hours for discharge.

documents_for_reimbursement: Discharge summary with diagnosis and treatment codes
Bank account details for NEFT transfer
Primary insurer settlement letter (for secondary COB claims)
claim_rules.contribution_clause: As secondary insurer: accepts claim for balance amount after primary insurer has settled its share. Applies own deductible (₹25,000 per claim) and coinsurance (75%) to the residual balance. Does not reimburse amount already paid by primary insurer.
claim_rules.source: IRDAI Master Circular on Protection of Policyholders Interests 2024. ManipalCigna blog: claims settled within 30 days; 2% above bank rate for delays. Niva Bupa in-house claim settlement: https://www.nivabupa.com/

coordination_of_benefits.irdai_framework: Indemnity Policy COB Rules per IRDAI (Insurance Products) Regulations 2024
coordination_of_benefits.role_in_aarav_claim: Secondary insurer for Aarav's ACL surgery. Receives residual balance after Insurer1 (primary) settles its share.
coordination_of_benefits.role_in_priya_claim: Primary insurer for Priya's physiotherapy bills (policyholder's choice).
coordination_of_benefits.secondary_insurer_deductible_treatment: When acting as secondary insurer, the per-claim deductible of ₹25,000 is applied to the residual balance passed from the primary insurer, not to the original total claim. This reflects standard IRDAI contribution clause interpretation.
coordination_of_benefits.no_profit_principle: Total reimbursement across both insurers cannot exceed actual admissible expenses.
coordination_of_benefits.policyholder_obligation: Policyholder must disclose all active policies at the time of claim. Non-disclosure may lead to claim rejection per policy terms.
coordination_of_benefits.insurer_coordination_obligation: If acting as primary insurer and claim exceeds sum insured, must coordinate with secondary insurer proactively.
coordination_of_benefits.source: IRDAI Health Department official FAQ: 'The primary Insurer shall seek the details of other available policies and coordinate with other Insurers.' (https://irdai.gov.in/health-dept). IRDAI (Insurance Products) Regulations 2024, Master Circular 29 May 2024, IRDAI/HLT/CIR/PRO/84/5/2024.
